name: ui-ux-designer
description: Create interface designs, wireframes, and design systems. Masters
capabilities: Create comprehensive design systems with atomic design methodology and design tokens
Plan and execute user research including interviews, usability testing, and A/B testing
Design WCAG 2.1/2.2 AA and AAA compliant interfaces with accessibility audits
Build component libraries with Figma Variables, Auto Layout, and Variants
Develop user journey maps, personas, and information architecture
Design cross-platform interfaces for web, mobile, desktop, and emerging devices
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a prompt-only skill containing no executable code, scripts, or external dependencies. Static analysis detected zero security patterns. The skill provides UI/UX design guidance and best practices as an AI persona. No network access, filesystem operations, or command execution capabilities present. Safe for publication.
valueStatement: Teams struggle with inconsistent UI/UX design and accessibility compliance across products. This skill provides expert guidance on design systems, user research, and inclusive design practices to create cohesive, accessible interfaces.
userTitle: Design accessible interfaces and scalable design systems
limitations: Cannot directly create visual designs or export design files - provides guidance only
Requires access to design tools like Figma for hands-on implementation
User research execution requires real users and cannot be simulated
Accessibility testing should be validated with actual assistive technologies

useCases.targetUser: Design leads establishing design infrastructure for startups or enterprise products
useCases.title: Build a Design System from Scratch
useCases.description: Create a comprehensive design system with design tokens, component library, and documentation for a new product or rebrand initiative.

useCases.targetUser: Product teams ensuring compliance with accessibility regulations and inclusive design standards
useCases.title: Accessibility Audit and Remediation
useCases.description: Conduct WCAG compliance audit of existing interfaces and create prioritized remediation strategy for accessibility improvements.

useCases.targetUser: UX researchers and product managers validating design decisions with user data
useCases.title: User Research Planning and Execution
useCases.description: Design comprehensive user research plans including interview guides, testing protocols, and analysis frameworks for product discovery.

promptTemplates.title: Beginner: Design Feedback Request
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this interface design and provide feedback on usability, accessibility, and visual hierarchy. Include specific improvement suggestions with WCAG compliance notes.
promptTemplates.scenario: Quick design review for early-stage mockups or existing interfaces needing optimization

promptTemplates.title: Intermediate: Design System Component Creation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a design token structure and component specification for a [component type]. Include color tokens, typography, spacing, states, variants, and accessibility requirements following atomic design principles.
promptTemplates.scenario: Building systematic, reusable components for a design system with developer handoff in mind

promptTemplates.title: Advanced: User Research Plan
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a comprehensive user research plan for [product/context]. Include research objectives, participant criteria, recruitment strategy, interview/discussion guide, tasks and scenarios, success metrics, and analysis framework. Consider accessibility and inclusive recruitment.
promptTemplates.scenario: Planning rigorous user research studies to inform major product decisions or redesigns

promptTemplates.title: Expert: Accessibility Audit and Remediation
promptTemplates.prompt: Conduct a WCAG 2.2 AA/AAA audit of [interface/product]. Identify all accessibility barriers, categorize by severity and WCAG criterion, explain user impact for each disability type, and create a prioritized remediation roadmap with implementation guidance.
promptTemplates.scenario: Comprehensive accessibility compliance audit for products requiring regulatory compliance or inclusive design

bestPractices: Always start with user needs and accessibility requirements before visual design decisions
Use design tokens as the single source of truth for consistency across platforms and teams
Document design decisions with rationale to enable future iteration and team alignment
antiPatterns: Designing components in isolation without considering system-wide consistency
Skipping user research and making assumptions about user needs and behaviors
Treating accessibility as a final checklist instead of integrating from the start

faq.question: Can this skill create actual visual designs or Figma files?
faq.answer: No, this skill provides expert guidance, specifications, and best practices. It cannot directly create or export Figma files, but it helps you plan and execute design work effectively in your design tools.

faq.question: Does this skill work for both web and mobile design?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill covers cross-platform design including responsive web, native iOS and mobile apps, desktop applications, and emerging device interfaces with platform-specific guidelines.

faq.question: Can this help with accessibility compliance for legal requirements?
faq.answer: This skill provides comprehensive WCAG 2.1/2.2 guidance and audit frameworks, but legal compliance should be validated with certified accessibility specialists and actual assistive technology testing.

faq.question: How does this skill handle design system versioning?
faq.answer: The skill provides guidance on version control strategies for design systems including semantic versioning, changelog practices, branching strategies, and release management workflows.

faq.question: Can this skill help with stakeholder presentations?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill includes collaboration and communication guidance including design storytelling, presentation techniques, stakeholder alignment strategies, and design critique facilitation.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for enterprise design teams?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill covers enterprise-scale challenges including multi-brand design systems, cross-team coordination, design governance, scaling design operations, and organizational design maturity.
